name: Tony Stark
financial_goals: Retire by 55 with 10Cr corpus
Build emergency fund of 6 months expenses
life_stage: Early career, single, just landed a role at a Bangalore tech startup — turned down three offers to take this one
preferences: Open to high-risk equity; interested in tech and pharma sectors

name: Hermione Granger
financial_goals: Children's higher education (15 years away)
Own a house within 5 years
Build retirement corpus of 5Cr
life_stage: Mid-career, married with one toddler, dual income household in Mumbai — maintains a spreadsheet for every financial goal
preferences: Prefers SIP route; avoids sectoral concentration

name: Atticus Finch
financial_goals: Daughter's wedding fund (10 years, 30L target)
Son's IIT/abroad education (5 years, 50L target)
Early retirement at 55 with 3Cr additional corpus
life_stage: Senior professional in Chennai, two school-age children, home loan EMI running — measured, principled, never cuts corners
preferences: Wants mix of growth and stability; comfortable with large-cap and flexi-cap funds

name: Minerva McGonagall
financial_goals: Preserve capital and generate regular income post-retirement in 5 years
Leave inheritance of 2Cr for each of two children
Healthcare contingency fund of 50L
life_stage: Pre-retirement, children settled, no liabilities, living in Kochi — has been planning this chapter meticulously since age 40
preferences: Capital preservation priority; prefers debt and hybrid funds; avoids small-cap

name: Gordon Gekko
financial_goals: FIRE (Financial Independence Retire Early) by age 45
Build portfolio of 15Cr
Fund a startup in 10 years
life_stage: Tech lead at a Pune-based MNC, single, high disposable income — greed, for lack of a better word, is good
preferences: Maximise equity exposure; interested in international diversification and small-cap alpha

name: Katniss Everdeen
financial_goals: Build emergency fund of 3 months salary
Save 5L for marriage in 6 years
Long-term wealth building
life_stage: First job, salaried software tester in Jaipur, lives frugally and sends money home — every rupee counts
preferences: Prefers simple, easy-to-understand investments; wants to start with a small amount and increase SIP annually

name: Jay Gatsby
financial_goals: Build 5Cr corpus in 10 years
Fund luxury apartment purchase in 3 years
Financial independence by 45
life_stage: High-earning sales director at a Mumbai fintech, single, lifestyle inflation is real — wants his money to work as hard as he does
preferences: Willing to take high risk for high reward; comfortable with volatility; interested in mid-cap and small-cap

name: Sherlock Holmes
financial_goals: Maximise real returns above inflation
Retire at 55 with 8Cr corpus
Fund daughter's foreign university education in 10 years
life_stage: Independent consultant in Delhi, obsessively data-driven, reads quarterly reports as a hobby — has a thesis on every fund in his portfolio
preferences: Wants funds with a strong, evidence-based performance track record; comfortable with detailed fund analysis; expects clear rationale for every recommendation

name: Elizabeth Bennet
financial_goals: Own a home in 5 years (target 60L down payment)
Child's education fund (15-year horizon)
Retirement corpus of 4Cr
life_stage: Mid-level IT professional in Hyderabad, recently married, joint home loan with spouse — practical and deliberate about every major decision
preferences: Tax-efficient investing (ELSS); prefers diversified equity over thematic; dislikes complexity

name: Forrest Gump
financial_goals: Retirement corpus of 2Cr by age 60
Children's school and college fees
Emergency fund of 6 months
life_stage: Factory supervisor in Coimbatore, steady but modest income, started investing seriously at 42 after a financial scare — simple goals, steady hands
preferences: Wants simple, set-and-forget portfolio; prefers stable, well-established funds; avoids anything complicated he cannot explain to his wife

name: Marge Simpson
financial_goals: Ensure children's education is fully funded (8 and 12 years away)
Preserve and modestly grow inherited corpus
Generate some income to supplement salary
life_stage: School teacher in Bengaluru, recently widowed and now sole earner — protecting what she has matters far more than chasing returns
preferences: Capital safety first; comfortable with balanced/hybrid funds; wants some regular income component

name: Ron Swanson
financial_goals: Maintain real value of corpus through retirement
Generate monthly income of 1L post-retirement at 60
Avoid any product he doesn't fully understand
life_stage: Business owner in Nagpur, distrusts financial institutions on principle but grudgingly accepts mutual funds — most of his wealth is in land and gold
preferences: Deeply conservative; favours short-duration debt, arbitrage, and gold funds; hard pass on any equity fund with 'opportunity' in its name

name: Tyrion Lannister
financial_goals: Children's education abroad (12 and 15 years away)
Retire comfortably at 55 with 8Cr
Build a 1Cr emergency buffer
life_stage: Senior product manager at an FMCG company in Gurugram, sharp mind and sharper tongue — reads fund factsheets the way others read novels
preferences: Appreciates nuanced allocation; likes flexi-cap and international diversification; wants rationale, not just recommendations

name: Walter Mitty
financial_goals: Retirement corpus of 3Cr by 62
Fund one dream international trip each year post-retirement
Leave some corpus for children
life_stage: Mid-level government contractor in Bhopal, spent his 30s and 40s dreaming of adventure and forgot to invest — making up for lost time, realistically
preferences: Prefers balanced funds; wants to catch up without taking unnecessary risk; SWP option for post-retirement income appeals

name: Scarlett O'Hara
financial_goals: Daughter's wedding fund of 25L in 4 years
Retirement income of 80K/month at 60
Preserve and transfer family wealth
life_stage: Runs a successful textile business in Surat, income is seasonal and irregular — fiercely self-reliant, never again wants to worry about money
preferences: Irregular lump sum investments preferred over fixed SIP; wants stable, predictable returns; conservative hybrid and short-duration debt

name: Albus Dumbledore
financial_goals: Systematic income of 1.5L/month from corpus
Grandchildren's education fund (20-25 year horizon for the children)
Estate planning — clean, tax-efficient wealth transfer
life_stage: Retired professor in Mysuru, draws pension, invests surplus from consulting — long view on everything, the portfolio included
preferences: Capital protection paramount; interested in SWP from debt/hybrid funds; wants estate planning angle built into the portfolio

name: Phoebe Buffay
financial_goals: Emergency fund of 3 months income
Marriage or travel fund in 5 years
Long-term wealth — no fixed target, just grow
life_stage: Freelance UX designer in Pune, income varies month to month — has accepted that a fixed SIP might not always go through, needs flexibility
preferences: Low fixed commitment; wants to top up when flush; prefers simple 2-3 fund portfolio over complex allocation

name: Sheldon Cooper
financial_goals: Retire at 52 with 12Cr portfolio — calculated to the last rupee
Fund own research institute or sabbatical
Maximise risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio, not raw return)
life_stage: IIT-M associate professor and startup advisor in Chennai, very high income relative to expenses — treats portfolio construction as an optimisation problem
preferences: Data-driven, evidence-based fund selection; international diversification; wants full quantitative rationale for every recommendation; expects each fund to justify its place with hard numbers

name: Don Corleone
financial_goals: Transfer 5Cr to each of three children tax-efficiently
Generate 2L/month passive income for family expenses
Protect corpus from inflation over 10-year horizon
life_stage: Business patriarch in Ahmedabad, third-generation family enterprise — the portfolio is for the family, not just himself, and he expects it to outlast him
preferences: Wealth preservation and transfer; large-cap, arbitrage, and short-duration debt; no speculation; wants everything structured for smooth succession

name: Leslie Knope
financial_goals: Supplement government pension with 3Cr personal corpus
Buy a second property in 8 years
Children's education fund
life_stage: State government officer in Delhi, pension-assured but not complacent — has a five-year plan, a ten-year plan, and a binder for each
preferences: ELSS for tax saving; balanced equity-debt mix; likes being shown long-term projections with numbers, not just words

name: Indiana Jones
financial_goals: Retire at 65 with enough to live comfortably without pension
Son's MBA fund (3 years away, 25L target)
Fund one big travel experience per year in retirement
life_stage: University professor in Kolkata with consulting income on the side — always busy, rarely sits still, started investing late but catches up with intensity
preferences: Flexi-cap and multi-asset; wants growth but nervous about volatility as retirement nears; open to dynamic allocation

name: Captain Haddock
financial_goals: Build 8Cr corpus by retirement
Buy land and build a house on return to India
Fund daughter's education abroad
life_stage: Merchant navy officer from Visakhapatnam, earns well but is at sea 8 months a year — invests in large lump sums when ashore, SIP runs in between
preferences: Comfortable with high equity exposure; prefers index + small-cap satellite; lump sum investments preferred alongside SIP

name: Jo March
financial_goals: Financial independence within 15 years — not FIRE, just freedom
Emergency fund equivalent to 6 months rent
Fund own creative venture in 8 years
life_stage: Journalist and content creator in Mumbai, modest but growing income — independent by conviction, invests every month without fail
preferences: Simple, transparent portfolio; avoids anything with heavy exit loads or unnecessary complexity; wants to fully understand every fund she owns

name: Remy
financial_goals: Build investing habit before any specific target
Accumulate 5L in 4 years as foundation
Retire comfortably — specific corpus TBD as income grows
life_stage: Fresh engineering graduate, first job in Coimbatore at ₹4.5L CTC — tiny surplus now, dreams large, knows compounding is on his side if he starts today
preferences: Minimum complexity; wants to start with just 1-2 funds and build from there; wants to step up SIP by 10% each year; mobile-app-friendly investing (Groww / Kuvera)

name: Nick Carraway
financial_goals: Re-enter Indian MF market after 10 years abroad
Retire in India at 62 with 5Cr corpus
Children's school fees for next 8 years
life_stage: NRI returning to Bengaluru after a decade in the US — has overseas savings, a modest corpus in Indian funds, and needs to rebuild his India-specific financial plan
preferences: Wants to understand India-specific products again; interested in international + domestic balanced approach; cautious about re-entry timing
